title: DataNex Website — Full Rebuild with 3+ Years Experience, 2023 Founded, and Enhanced Features

requirements.text: Update all references to experience from '2–3 years' to '3+ years' and all references to founding year to '2023' throughout every section of the site (hero badge, about section, hero stat panel, footer, meta descriptions, and any other mentions).

quotes: set 3+ years of expirience and founded in 2023

acceptanceCriteria: Hero stat panel shows '3+' for years experience
About section text reads '3+ years of hands-on experience'
Footer reads 'Founded 2023'
No instance of '2–3 years' remains anywhere on the site

requirements.text: Rebuild the Hero section to be more visually striking: replace the plain initials placeholder with an animated data-visualization motif (live-drawing SVG lines suggesting a scraping pipeline), add a typewriter effect cycling through specialties ('Web Scraping', 'Data Research', 'Lead Generation', 'Market Intelligence'), keep the animated particle canvas, stats panel, ticker, and scroll hint.

quotes: build original unique and amazing website

acceptanceCriteria: Typewriter cycling text is visible beneath the main headline
Animated SVG pipeline motif renders in the hero background or alongside the headline
Particle canvas, stats panel, ticker, and scroll hint remain functional
Hero is fully responsive on mobile

requirements.text: Add a 'Live Data Feed' marquee/ticker strip between the Hero and About sections that displays real-time-style animated data rows (mock records streaming in) with columns like Source, Records, Status, Accuracy — styled in the navy/gold theme using monospace font to feel like a live terminal output.

quotes: build original unique and amazing website

acceptanceCriteria: Ticker strip appears between hero and about sections
Rows animate in continuously (looped)
Columns include at least: Source, Records, Status, Accuracy
Uses monospace font and gold/green accent colors matching the theme

requirements.text: Redesign the About section to include a professional profile card on the left side that shows: initials avatar with an animated gold ring border, name 'Jay Gohil', title 'Data Research Specialist & Founder', founding badge '2023', a row of platform badges (Upwork, Fiverr, LinkedIn icons), and the 99.2% accuracy deco card. Remove the old plain frame placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile card renders with animated gold ring around avatar
Name, title, and founding year badges are visible
Platform badges row (Upwork, Fiverr, LinkedIn) is present
99.2% accuracy card is retained

requirements.text: Add an interactive 'Data Scope Estimator' tool section between the Services and Projects sections. Users select: Target Website Type (dropdown: E-commerce, Directory, Social, News, Custom), Volume (slider: 1K–1M records), Output Format (CSV / Excel / JSON / MySQL), and Frequency (One-time / Weekly / Monthly). The tool dynamically displays an estimated delivery timeline and complexity badge (Simple / Moderate / Complex / Enterprise) with a CTA button 'Request This Dataset →' linking to the contact form.

quotes: build original unique and amazing website

acceptanceCriteria: All four input controls are interactive and functional
Estimated delivery time updates dynamically as inputs change
Complexity badge updates based on volume and frequency selections
CTA button scrolls to the contact section
Section is fully responsive

requirements.text: Enhance the Projects section with a 'View All' filter tab bar (All / E-commerce / B2B Research / Lead Gen / Automation) above the project grid. Clicking a filter tab shows only matching project cards with a smooth CSS transition. Keep existing 3 projects and add 2 more: one for 'Real Estate Listing Intelligence' (property data scraping) and one for 'News & Media Sentiment Dataset' (news scraping + sentiment tagging).
acceptanceCriteria: Filter tabs appear above the project grid
Clicking a tab filters visible cards with animation
Two new project cards appear in the grid
'All' tab shows all 5 projects
Each new card includes category, title, description, result metric, and tech chips

requirements.text: Add a 'Client Testimonials' section between the Why Choose Us section and the Counter Strip. Display 4 testimonial cards in a horizontally scrollable carousel. Each card shows: client name, company, country flag emoji, star rating (5 stars), a quote, and a key metric (e.g., '250K records delivered'). Include prev/next arrow buttons and dot indicators. Auto-advance every 5 seconds.

quotes: build original unique and amazing website

acceptanceCriteria: Testimonial section renders with at least 4 cards
Carousel auto-advances every 5 seconds
Prev/next buttons and dot indicators are functional
Each card shows name, company, flag, stars, quote, and a metric
Section is responsive (single card view on mobile)

requirements.text: Add a 'Pricing & Packages' section after the Testimonials section, featuring three tiered cards: Starter ($49 — up to 5K records, CSV, 3-day delivery), Professional ($149 — up to 50K records, CSV/Excel/JSON, 5-day delivery, MySQL storage), and Enterprise (Custom — 100K+ records, all formats, dedicated pipeline, ongoing support). Each card shows features list, a highlighted badge for the recommended plan, and a CTA button. Include a note: 'All prices in USD. Custom quotes available for complex projects.'
acceptanceCriteria: Three pricing cards render with correct tier names, prices, and features
Professional plan is visually highlighted as recommended
Each card has a CTA button linking to the contact section
USD note and custom quote mention are present
Section is responsive (stacked on mobile)

requirements.text: Add an animated 'Terminal / Code Window' decorative component in the Technologies section (right-side panel area), showing a mock Python scraping script that types itself out character by character in a loop. The terminal window should have a macOS-style title bar (red/yellow/green dots), dark background, and syntax-highlighted code lines using the gold and slate color palette.

quotes: build original unique and amazing website

acceptanceCriteria: Terminal window component renders with macOS-style title bar dots
Code types itself out character-by-character in an infinite loop
Syntax highlighting uses gold for keywords and slate/green for comments/strings
Component is positioned in or near the Technologies section

requirements.text: Add a sticky floating 'Quick Contact' pill button that appears after scrolling 300px, distinct from the existing floating contact button. It should show the text 'Get a Free Quote' with a gold background and slide in from the bottom-right. Clicking it smoothly scrolls to the contact section.
acceptanceCriteria: Button is hidden on initial load and appears after 300px scroll
Button has gold background with navy text, pill shape
Clicking it scrolls to the #contact section
Does not overlap or conflict with other fixed UI elements
Visible on both desktop and mobile

requirements.text: Update the contact form to store submitted messages in the Motoko backend. The backend actor should expose a submitContactMessage function that accepts name, email, company, budget, service, and message fields, and stores them in a stable array. Also expose a getMessages query function (admin use, not gated) that returns all stored messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact form submission calls the backend submitContactMessage function
Backend stores submitted data in a stable array across upgrades
getMessages returns all stored messages
Frontend shows the same success/error toast after submission
Form resets on successful submission

requirements.text: Update the Counter Strip section data to reflect the correct values aligned with 3+ years experience and 2023 founding: Records Scraped: 2.4M+, Projects Delivered: 60+, Data Accuracy Rate: 99.2%, Industries Served: 7+. Ensure all counter animations trigger correctly on scroll into view.

quotes: set 3+ years of expirience and founded in 2023

acceptanceCriteria: Counter strip shows exactly these four metrics with correct values
All counters animate from 0 to target value on scroll
Gold background counter strip is visually consistent with design

requirements.text: Update the footer copyright line to read '© 2023–2026 DataNex Research Solutions · Jay Gohil · All Rights Reserved' and ensure the footer 'Founded 2023' badge appears in the brand column below the logo. Verify all footer navigation links point to the correct section anchors.
acceptanceCriteria: Copyright reads '© 2023–2026 DataNex Research Solutions'
Founded 2023 badge is visible in the footer brand column
All footer nav links resolve to correct page sections

requirements.text: Ensure full mobile responsiveness across all new and existing sections. All new components (Data Scope Estimator, Testimonials Carousel, Pricing Cards, Terminal Window, Live Data Feed ticker, Project Filter tabs) must be tested for layout at 375px, 768px, and 1200px breakpoints. Use Tailwind responsive prefixes consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal overflow at 375px viewport width
All new sections stack vertically on mobile
Carousel shows single card on mobile
Pricing cards stack vertically on mobile
Terminal window scales down gracefully on small screens

constraints: Maintain the existing navy (#060F1E) and gold (#C9A84C) color palette throughout all new components
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths)
Do not modify frontend/src/main.tsx or frontend/src/components/ui/
All new sections must follow the existing reveal/scroll-animation pattern
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Real backend email sending (no SMTP or email service integration)
External API integrations (no third-party data APIs)

required: Dark navy background with subtle gold geometric data-grid lines and faint circuit node dots, landscape banner (hero-bg-pattern.dim_1920x1080.png)
Professional dark-themed logo mark with 'DN' initials in gold on deep navy circle, square icon format (datanex-logo-icon.dim_256x256.png)
Abstract gold-on-navy data flow illustration showing connected nodes and lines suggesting a web scraping pipeline (data-pipeline-illustration.dim_800x600.png)
